headline: Cambodia Reaches Historic HIV Milestone in Asia-Pacific
meta_description: Cambodia becomes the first country in Asia and the Pacific to meet the UN's most demanding HIV targets, achieving 95% treatment success.
summary_short: Cambodia has achieved what no other Asian or Pacific nation has: meeting the UN's toughest HIV targets with 95% of people living with HIV knowing their status and receiv…
body:
Cambodia has become the first country in Asia and the Pacific to reach the UN's most ambitious HIV targets—a landmark achievement in regional public health.

The milestone reflects a remarkable commitment: 95% of people living with HIV now know their status, are receiving treatment, and have achieved viral suppression. This three-part target represents the gold standard for HIV response, requiring not just diagnosis, but sustained access to medication and medical oversight.

The accomplishment matters because it shows what coordinated effort can achieve. Asia and the Pacific, home to over half the world's population, has long faced significant health challenges. Cambodia's success demonstrates that meeting the UN's toughest health benchmarks is possible even in regions facing resource constraints.

For the millions still affected by HIV globally, Cambodia's progress offers concrete hope—proof that comprehensive treatment targets aren't aspirational, but achievable.
